animated_objects.description: Button dips then returns on press.
animated_objects.motion_summary: The refill button shows a classic tap-feedback scale dip. It contracts to approximately 95% uniformly on X and Y over 0.15 seconds, then returns to full scale by 0.30 seconds. No position change is observed -- the 3.2px CV displacement is below the jitter threshold. The Z scale stays constant at 1.0. This is a standard mobile button feedback pattern.

relationships.explanation: Tapping the refill button triggers the heart fill sequence. The button dip provides immediate tactile feedback while the hearts begin their fill animation after the dip completes.

source_evidence.match_reason: Track_3's bounding box [380,1420,720,1560] overlaps with btn_refill_green detection box [370,1410,710,1570] at approximately 91% IoU. The normalized center positions are nearly identical (track: 0.509/0.776, detection: 0.500/0.776). The track shows a clear scale oscillation from 1.0 to 0.94 and back to 1.0, consistent with tap feedback. Track_7 was rejected because its bbox (16x15px) is far too small for this 340x160px button.
visual_evidence: large green rounded rectangular button at the bottom center of the popup card
button occupies roughly 340x160 pixels in a 1080x1920 frame
visible scale contraction in annotated frames 15-17
no position change -- button stays anchored at bottom center

animated_objects.description: Empty heart fills to full state with a scale pop.
animated_objects.motion_summary: The leftmost heart shows a scale pop when it fills. It expands to 115% over 0.10 seconds then settles back to 100% by 0.25 seconds. The fill state change (empty sprite to full sprite) happens at the peak of the scale pop. No position translation is observed. This animation repeats identically for heart_fill_1 and heart_fill_2 with staggered start times.

relationships.explanation: This heart is the first in a row of three hearts that fill sequentially from left to right. Each heart animates identically but starts 0.15 seconds after the previous one.

source_evidence.match_reason: Track_5 bbox [420,820,480,880] overlaps with the first heart icon detection box. The scale oscillation from 1.0 to 1.18 matches a fill-pop pattern. Confidence is moderate (0.78) because the three hearts are similarly sized and close together, making exact track-to-heart assignment somewhat ambiguous -- track_5 was assigned to the leftmost heart based on its x-coordinate being the smallest of the three heart-region tracks.
visual_evidence: small heart-shaped icon, leftmost of three in a horizontal row
changes from grey/empty appearance to red/filled during animation
brief scale pulse visible in annotated frames 18-23
located in the upper-center area of the popup card

animated_objects.description: Dark overlay fades from visible to invisible when popup dismisses.
animated_objects.motion_summary: The dark semi-transparent overlay behind the popup card holds steady at 60% opacity for the first 1.2 seconds (while the refill animation sequence plays), then fades to fully transparent over 0.6 seconds as the popup dismisses. The overlay covers the full screen and has no position or scale changes. The fade timing was inferred from the last frame showing no overlay, combined with the overall animation sequence duration.

source_evidence.match_reason: No CV track was produced for this object because the overlay is a uniform dark semi-transparent layer -- the CV pipeline cannot track uniform-color regions. The overlay's existence and fade behavior were inferred by comparing the first frame (dark overlay visible behind popup) with the last frame (no overlay, background fully exposed). The fade timing of 0.6 seconds was estimated from the timestamp of the last popup-related animation ending.
visual_evidence: dark semi-transparent layer covering entire screen behind popup card
visible in frame_first.png as a darkened background
absent in frame_last.png -- background is fully bright
no CV track produced due to uniform color

animation_sequences.description: Button tap -> hearts fill sequentially -> overlay dismiss.

scene_wiring.note: Minimal hints for unity-animation-builder to create/find supporting GameObjects.

unmatched_tracks.reason: Tiny bbox (16x15px), only 4 frames. Likely a small badge artifact or noise. Too small to match any detection object.
Edge artifact near screen boundary, 3 frames, no spatial overlap with any detection object.

inferred_objects.reason: Uniform dark overlay -- CV cannot track flat-color regions. Inferred from first vs last frame visual comparison.

not_in_clip.reason: Close button is present in detection JSON but not visible in the video clip. The video starts after the popup is already open and ends before any close interaction.
